area: Cross-repository planning authority is remembered but not enforced before authoring
finding: A Captain working in the framework repository created a complete framework-local optimization plan even though the established planning authority was 3KLife and only the imported ATM ledger belonged in the framework. The mistake recurred because handoff memory and a general planning/target distinction did not force an explicit authority declaration before file creation.
expectedBehavior: Before authoring, ATM guidance must resolve and state planning, target, and closure authority. When they differ, the full plan and source cards stay in the planning repository; the target accepts only CLI-imported ledger records and explicit neutral product deliverables.
evidenceOrRepro: Reproduced during the 2026-07-14 governance-optimization planning turn; the local plan was removed and replaced by a Traditional Chinese plan plus `tasks/` under the coordinating planning repository.
followUp: Guidance added to source and installed `atm-task-card-authoring` / `atm-dispatch` skills. Add a deterministic authoring/import preflight under `ATM-GOVOPT-008` so source-path authority is machine-checked rather than memory-dependent.
